name: T3 Code

form_factor.comment: A minimal GUI for coding agents: launch via CLI (npx t3@latest), a web interface, and desktop apps for macOS/Windows/Linux. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://github.com/pingdotgg/t3code; Web: https://t3.codes; Desktop App: https://t3.codes.

released_in.comment: Early-stage open-source project from Theo / the T3 team; still pre-1.0.

pricing.value: Free, open source (MIT); bring your own API keys
pricing.comment: MIT-licensed and free; you pay only for the model usage your own accounts/keys accrue (BYOK).

some_free_usage.comment: The app is free, but real agent work is billed to your own API keys.

no_account_required.comment: Requires credentials for an underlying agent (Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, or OpenCode).

can_use_other_subscriptions.comment: BYOK: connects to Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, or OpenCode with the credentials you already have.

rules.comment: Repository ships an AGENTS.md; underlying-agent rule files (AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md) apply through the selected harness.

skills.comment: No public documentation on reusable skills; capabilities come from the selected agent.

mcp_servers.comment: No MCP support documented for the GUI itself; depends on the underlying agent.

monitor.comment: GUI surfaces the status of agents running across worktrees, but there is no scriptable background monitor/watch primitive.

model_selection.comment: Model selector supports multiple providers from a single interface.

approval_mode.comment: Full-access mode edits without prompting; supervised mode requires approval for each action.

sandbox_mode.comment: Isolation comes from per-task git worktrees; no dedicated network/process sandbox documented.

non_interactive.comment: GUI-first; a remote-backend/headless mode is planned but not yet available.

git_integration.comment: Per-task git worktrees with git diff review and merge-when-confirmed workflow.

hosted_agent.comment: Runs locally; no managed cloud execution platform.

custom_model_provider.comment: Limited to supported harnesses (Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode) and their providers, not arbitrary OpenAI-compatible endpoints.

git_worktrees.comment: Native git worktree support: each task runs in its own worktree/directory, reviewed with git diff and merged only when confirmed.

done_notifications.comment: No public documentation on completion/attention notifications.

byok_prompt_caching.comment: Drives the real Codex / Claude Code harnesses on your own API keys, so their native provider prompt caching still applies.

notes: Open-source (MIT) minimal GUI/CLI bridge that runs Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, or OpenCode in native git worktrees using your own API keys (BYOK).
